# terrible_community_hrobot_firewall (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `allowlist_hos` (Boolean) Whether Hetzner services have access.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `filter_ipv6` (Boolean) Whether to filter IPv6 traffic as well. IPv4 traffic is always filtered, IPv6 traffic filtering needs to be explicitly enabled.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `port` (String) Switch port of firewall.
- `rules` (String) Firewall rules.
- `server_ip` (String) The server's main IP address. Exactly one of `server_ip` and `server_number` must be specified. Note that Hetzner deprecated identifying the server's firewall by the server's main IP. Using this option can thus stop working at any time in the future. Use `server_number` instead.
- `server_number` (Number) The server's number. Exactly one of `server_ip` and `server_number` must be specified.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Status of the firewall. Firewall is active if state is `present`, and disabled if state is `absent`.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `update_timeout` (Number) Timeout to use when configuring the firewall. Note that the API call returns before the firewall has been successfully set up.
- `wait_delay` (Number) Delay to wait (in seconds) before checking again whether the firewall has been configured.
- `wait_for_configured` (Boolean) Whether to wait until the firewall has been successfully configured before determining what to do, and before returning from the module. The API returns status `in progress` when the firewall is currently being configured. If this happens, the module will try again until the status changes to `active` or `disabled`. Please note that there is a request limit. If you have to do multiple updates, it can be better to disable waiting, and regularly use `community.hrobot.firewall_info` to query status.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `firewall` (String) The firewall configuration.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
